ops.text: cc-interact's consume-cursor persistence races on shutdown: killing a `cco fleet watch` (human mode, registered consumer `fleet-watch`) logs `rename …/fleet-watch.cursor.tmp …/fleet-watch.cursor: no such file or directory` to stderr on Ctrl-C — the tmp file is gone by the time the rename runs, likely a ctx-cancel vs cursor-flush ordering issue in cc-interact's consume package. Output is unaffected; pure shutdown noise. Fix belongs upstream in cc-interact (consume.ConsumeEvents cursor write path), not in cc-orchestrate.
